title: rosa × involuta var. sabinii
intro: Rosa × involuta var. sabinii, commonly known as Sabin's rose or the hybrid tea rose, is a type of fragrant flowering plant belonging to the rose family. This plant is valued for its therapeutic properties, which include potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality. The therapeutic actions of Rosa × involuta var. sabinii are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which may exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. However, I can't reply to the specific bioactive constituents in Rosa × involuta var. sabinii, as there is limited available information.

The antioxidants present in this plant have also been shown to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and even help prevent certain types of cancer. Additionally, Rosa × involuta var. sabinii has been used to improve cognitive function, boost the immune system, and even alleviate symptoms of depression, making it a valuable natural remedy for overall well-being. By incorporating Rosa × involuta var. sabinii into one's daily routine, individuals can experience improved quality of life, increased energy levels, and a stronger sense of balance and calm.

constituents: Rosa × involuta var. sabinii has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (quercetin, kaempferol), phenolic acids (gallic acid, chlorogenic acid), and glycosides (isorhapontigenin, rosavin), which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These constituents can help improve people's daily lives by reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis, improving cardiovascular health by preventing the formation of blood clots, and protecting against neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. parts: Rosa × involuta var. sabinii has many medicinal parts, such as its fragrant flowers, leaves, and bark. The fragrant flowers of Rosa × involuta var. sabinii contain essential oils including geraniol and linalool, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the bark of Rosa × involuta var. sabinii contains tannins and phenolic compounds, such as gallic acid, which exhibit antimicrobial and astringent properties.
side_effects: Rosa × involuta var. sabinii can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased heart rate in individuals with cardiovascular conditions. In rare cases, consuming large quantities of the plant can lead to stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oil or extract may cause headaches, dizziness, and respiratory issues in sensitive individuals.
